title: centrolepis strigosa subsp. rupestris
intro: Centrolepis strigosa subsp. rupestris is a rare, endemic species of flowering plant in the Centrolepidaceae family, specifically a type of sedge that is native to New Zealand. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Centrolepis strigosa subsp. rupestris typically involve the plant's leaves and stems being dried and made into teas, tinctures, or infusions for medicinal use.
